title: Bridge Paper: Revisiting the mental imagery debate: New evidence from aphantasia and neuroimaging.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Mental imagery — visual imagery as real perception, imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.
**Phase context:** mental imagery, visual imagery, imagination, aphantasia, mental simulation, pareidolia

Paper: Revisiting the mental imagery debate: New evidence from aphantasia and neuroimaging.
DOI: 10.1016/j.cortex.2025.09.011
Abstract: In this viewpoint letter, I critically examine the longstanding debate regarding the nature of mental imagery-specifically the tension between depictive and propositional theories-through the lens of recent neuroscientific findings. While early studies using neuroimaging were interpreted as supporting a depictive, perception-like model of visual imagery, emerging data from individuals with aphantasia present compelling counterevidence.
